anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: "LOW" is misleading — projections are $0 because no sources were discovered, not because no calls happen.**

(a) **Runaway loops:** 26 timers found, 0 attributed to Anthropic/Gemini. Discovery is broken (regex/import miss). A retry-on-exception loop inside any timer fires Opus repeatedly — the lockfile only blocks *re-entry across days*, not intra-day retries before the file is written.

(b) **Missing caps:** Anthropic cap is filesystem-based (`/tmp/...txt`) — wiped on reboot/container restart = unlimited fires. No per-hour or per-run cap. Gemini cap is in one helper; any direct SDK import bypasses it.

(c) **2× rate:** Opus at 2/day = ~$4 — trivial. But if loop defeats lockfile, 100×/day = ~$200/day.

(d) **Highest silent burn risk: `audit_fire_debate`** — multi-turn Opus
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks:**

(a) **Runaway loop**: /tmp lockfile can be deleted or ignored by concurrent trading-bot instances/container restarts → repeated Opus calls ($2 each). A 1-sec retry loop burns ~$7k/day.

(b) **Missing caps**: Gemini cap exists only in two audit functions; any new trading code using Gemini bypasses it. Haiku reintroduction has zero enforcement.

(c) **2x rate**: Anthropic at 2 calls/day = $4/day (harmless). Gemini at 200 calls/day still cheap.

(d) **Silent burner**: Anthropic Opus via audit_fire_debate. High per-call cost, lockfile failure invisible in normal trading logs.

Current projected $0 is misleading — the audit protection itself is the fragility.
